data.title: Missile Strikes Cause Power Outages in Tehran Amid Regional Tensions
data.description: Parts of Tehran experienced electrical power outages following missile strikes on Sunday. Bloomberg posted on X that the attacks were part of a series of assaults by Iran and its proxies targeting U.S. allies over the weekend. In response to the escalating tensions, thousands of additional American military personnel have been deployed to the region.

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks Face Volatility Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description:
Hong Kong stocks experienced a decline last week, falling below the 25,000-point mark due to geopolitical tensions. According to Ming Pao, Kwok Sze Chi, Vice Chairman of the Hong Kong Stock Analysts Association, noted that the ongoing unrest in the Middle East has contributed to the overall soft performance of the stock market in March. He suggested that in the absence of unexpected events, the market might maintain a fluctuating pattern.

In March, the market showed a trend of rising initially and then declining, with the Hang Seng Index dropping from a high of 26,403 points to a low of 22,403 points on the 23rd, marking a fluctuation of 2,200 points within the month. The focus now is whether the market can overcome the current challenges in April, with developments in the Middle East being a crucial factor.

data.title: Geopolitical Factors Influence Oil Prices and Strengthen Dollar, Affecting Yen Exchange Rate
data.description: Geopolitical factors have contributed to rising oil prices and a strengthening U.S. dollar, leading to a decline in the yen's exchange rate against the Hong Kong dollar, which fell below 4.9 last week. According to Ming Pao, the market generally anticipates limited further depreciation of the yen, with potential for a slight recovery later this year due to interest rate hikes. Individuals planning to travel to Japan this year might consider exchanging yen at banks through telegraphic transfer at current low rates or directly at currency exchange shops offering attractive rates.

data.title: U.S. Extends Deadline on Iran Energy Strikes Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has extended the deadline for potential strikes on Iranian energy facilities by 10 days, moving it to April 6, Eastern Time. According to Ming Pao, Trump stated that this decision was made at the request of the Iranian government. However, reports indicate that Iran has denied making such a request.

In related economic news, the Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) has significantly raised its inflation forecasts for major global economies. The OECD now estimates that the inflation rate in the United States could rise to 4.2% this year. The economic situation remains complex and volatile, with the U.S. Dollar Index likely to hover around 100 in the short term.

data.title: U.S.-Iran Tensions Impact Global Energy Prices
data.description:
The ongoing conflict between the United States and Iran has escalated into a war of attrition, as predicted last week. According to Ming Pao, despite any public statements by U.S. President Donald Trump regarding negotiations with Iran, the reality is that both nations are deeply entrenched in this conflict.

Iran, lacking air superiority, is unable to defend against airstrikes from the U.S. and Israel. Consequently, its primary leverage lies in the potential blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and attacks on oil infrastructure in other Gulf countries. Such actions could lead to a sharp increase in international energy prices, triggering inflation and interest rate hikes. This strategy aims to counter the heavily indebted United States, reminiscent of the United Kingdom's retreat during the Suez Canal Crisis in 1956.

data.title: Ethereum(ETH) Surpasses 2,000 USDT with a Narrowed 1.17% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 29, 2026, 20:20 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has crossed the 2,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 2,000.290039 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 1.17% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Congress Proposes Digital Asset PARITY Act to Extend Wash-Sale Rule
data.description: Congress has introduced the Digital Asset PARITY Act draft, aiming to apply the 30-day wash-sale rule to actively traded digital assets and related derivatives. According to NS3.AI, this legislative proposal also includes a specific tax exemption for regulated payment stablecoin sales within a $0.99-$1.01 per-unit range. The wash-sale rule extension would be effective immediately upon enactment, while the stablecoin provision is set to commence for taxable years starting after December 31, 2025. Congress is currently finalizing the details of this tax carveout.

data.title: Missile Strike in Southern Israel Causes Fire and Hazardous Material Leak
data.description: A missile attack on an industrial area in Beersheba, southern Israel, led to a fire and hazardous material leak on Sunday. According to RTHK, Israeli media reported that the Neot Hovav industrial zone, located south of Beersheba, was hit by an Iranian missile on Sunday afternoon, igniting a blaze. The Israeli Public Broadcasting Corporation cited fire and rescue personnel, stating that cleanup operations would continue for several hours and advised nearby individuals to remain in safe zones to avoid inhaling harmful substances in the air. The fire and rescue department has dispatched specialized hazardous materials teams to assess the danger level. Neot Hovav is a major industrial area in southern Israel, primarily involved in chemical production and hazardous waste management.

data.title: Wall Street Bond Managers Warn of Economic Risks from US-Iran Conflict
data.description: Some of Wall Street's leading bond-fund managers have expressed concerns that financial markets are underestimating the potential economic impact of the ongoing conflict between the United States and Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that these experts believe the tensions could lead to a significant slowdown in an already struggling economy. The bond managers emphasize the need for investors to consider the broader implications of geopolitical events on market stability and economic growth.

data.title: Strategy Pauses Bitcoin Accumulation After 13-Week Buying Streak
data.description: Strategy may have halted its bitcoin purchases last week, potentially concluding a 13-week period during which it acquired 90,831 BTC. According to NS3.AI, the company's dashboard indicates that Strategy currently holds 762,099 bitcoins, with an average acquisition price of $75,694 per token.

data.title: OnePay Expands Crypto Offerings with New Token Additions
data.description: Walmart-backed OnePay has expanded its cryptocurrency offerings by adding over a dozen new tokens to its platform, including SUI, Polygon, and Arbitrum. According to NS3.AI, the move aims to enhance the platform's appeal to users who are new to the crypto space. Ron Rojany, a representative from OnePay, noted that the platform is experiencing strong engagement from these newer users, although specific adoption figures were not disclosed.

data.title: Middle East Conflict: Regional Powers Back US-Iran Peace Talks
data.description: Foreign ministers from Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Egypt, and Turkey have expressed their support for peace talks between the United States and Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing efforts to address the escalating conflict in the Middle East, which has now entered its fifth week. The backing from these regional powers underscores the international community's desire for a diplomatic resolution to the crisis.

data.title: Donald Trump's Rise Rooted in Historical Traditions
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's ascent to power was not an overnight phenomenon marked by a dramatic entrance, such as descending a golden escalator. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that his rise was influenced by deep-seated traditions within U.S. history. These historical patterns have played a significant role in shaping the political landscape and are unlikely to vanish quickly. The enduring nature of these traditions suggests that the impact of Trump's presidency will continue to be felt in the future, reflecting the persistent influence of historical precedents in American politics.

data.title: ZachXBT Uncovers Network of X Accounts with Millions of Followers
data.description: Blockchain sleuth ZachXBT posted on X, revealing the discovery of a network comprising 16 X accounts. These accounts collectively boast a total of 2.23 million followers and are reportedly employing identical strategies in their operations. ZachXBT's investigation highlights the coordinated efforts among these accounts, raising concerns about their activities and potential impact on the platform. The findings underscore the need for vigilance and scrutiny in monitoring social media networks to ensure transparency and authenticity in digital interactions.

data.title: Israel Seeks Solutions for Worship Access at Church of the Holy Sepulchre
data.description: Israel is exploring options to facilitate worship at the Church of the Holy Sepulchre following an incident where police barred the Latin Patriarch from conducting mass due to security concerns. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing discussions aimed at resolving the situation. The Church of the Holy Sepulchre, a significant religious site, has been at the center of security-related challenges, prompting authorities to seek a balance between safety and religious practices. Efforts are underway to ensure that worshippers can access the site while addressing the security issues that led to the recent restrictions.

data.title: Rheinmetall Acknowledges Ukrainian Defense Efforts Amid Drone Remarks
data.description: Rheinmetall, a leading German defense company, has expressed its 'utmost respect' for the defense efforts of Ukrainians against Russia. This statement follows remarks made by the company's chief executive that seemed to downplay the use of drones in the conflict. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's acknowledgment of the significant role played by Ukrainian forces in their ongoing defense operations. Rheinmetall's comments come amid heightened tensions and continued military engagements in the region.

data.title: Ethereum's Supply Increases by Over 81,000 ETH in March
data.description: Ethereum's net supply has risen by 81,790.88 ETH over the past 30 days, according to BlockBeats. Data from Ultrasound.money indicates that the total supply of Ethereum has reached 121,570,566 ETH. The current annual growth rate of Ethereum's supply stands at 0.82%.

data.title: Countries Shift Away from US Dollar Amid Rising US Debt
data.description: Russia, China, Iran, and India are reportedly reducing their reliance on the US dollar as the U.S. national debt reaches $39 trillion. According to NS3.AI, these nations are increasingly utilizing alternatives such as the yuan, gold, barter systems, or local-currency settlements to diversify their reserves and conduct trade, moving away from the dollar.

data.title: CLARITY Act Draft May Impact DeFi Tokens, Says 10x Research
data.description: The latest draft of the CLARITY Act could pose challenges for DeFi tokens, according to 10x Research. The proposed legislation aims to prohibit yield or reward-like features on stablecoin balances. According to NS3.AI, Markus Thielen expressed concerns that this change might shift yield opportunities from crypto-native platforms to traditional financial institutions such as banks, money market funds, and regulated wrappers. This potential re-centralization could impact the decentralized finance sector significantly.

data.title: Ethereum Targets Glamsterdam and Hegota in 2026 After Major Upgrades
data.description: Ethereum has undergone five significant upgrades since transitioning to a proof-of-stake model in 2022. According to NS3.AI, these upgrades have focused on enhancing various aspects of the network. The Dencun upgrade has successfully reduced layer-2 costs, while the Pectra and Fusaka upgrades have expanded staking and rollup data capacity. Looking ahead, Ethereum is aiming for further advancements with Glamsterdam and Hegota set for 2026.
